base_case: Bull (melt-up intact but cracking) — Golden Cross regime holds with price above both SMAs, but the 7-session ATH streak broke today on a gap-down/failed-recovery distribution day; 30–60 day horizon now carries a meaningful (30%) corrective-break risk toward the 23.6% Fib at 7,330.
trend_regime: Golden Cross intact: 50-day above 200-day, price above both. +10.1% premium to 50-day SMA and +14.6% premium to 200-day SMA — still extended, but premiums compressed slightly as price pulled back from the ATH.
valuation_bucket: 20–22x (Cautious) — Forward P/E 21.68x; buy only on Fib + SMA + sentiment confluence. ERP ~+0.112% remains functionally zero (ceiling-adjacent).

signals.bollinger_position: within band — price pulled away from upper band for the first time
signals.vix_regime: low / below 20 — no elevated risk flag

what_changed_today: ATH streak BROKEN — first non-ATH close since the 7-session run began; close 7,584.31 sits 36.6 pts below the June 2 cycle high of 7,620.90.
Distribution day: gap-down open to 7,516 (−93 pts), widest intraday range of the stretch (81.6 pts), and a green close that still failed to reclaim the June 2 close by 25 pts.
RSI fell sharply — sub-30 on the gap-down open, recovered only to ~62–65 at close, well below the 70+ that characterized ATH sessions; 5-day RSI now shows a clear lower peak.
MFI now forms three consecutive declining peaks vs flat/rising price — a classic triple bearish divergence (mid-May to today).
F&G fell to 55 (5th consecutive decline, −6 from May 26 peak of 61) even as SPX gained 65 pts net over the stretch.
Monte Carlo P(7,700 first vs 7,400) degraded from 66.6% to 60.6% — now BELOW the 65% threshold on this secondary contest.
New flag: P(23.6% Fib 7,330 visited within 60d) = 63.0% — 7,330 is now a more-likely-than-not destination.
Junk bond spread holds 1.45% for a 3rd consecutive day — sustained Extreme Fear credit warning, no longer a one-day anomaly.
New defensive tripwire added: close below 7,400 (without prior Wave 1) triggers a 10–15% partial defensive trim.
narrative_summary: The distribution signals have arrived: SPX broke its 7-session ATH streak with a gap-down open to 7,516, an 81-point range, and a green close that still failed to reclaim the prior close — the textbook signature of an exhausted rally. Every qualitative gauge has moved bearish for five straight sessions (F&G falling to 55, junk spreads pinned at 1.45%, breadth flat, RSI/MFI decelerating into a triple bearish divergence), while the primary Monte Carlo edge to Wave 1 stays strong at 77.3% but the secondary 7,700-vs-7,400 contest has degraded to 60.6%, below threshold. The methodology keeps the position long for now — Golden Cross intact, VIX ~15, Wave 1 not reached — so the disciplined stance is HOLD with the GTC trim at 7,696 live and a new defensive tripwire: a close below 7,400 triggers a 10–15% partial trim. The ATH at 7,620 is now resistance rather than a floor, and the unresolved credit/equity divergence remains the dominant non-technical risk.
open_questions: Does the junk bond spread post a 4th consecutive session at 1.45%+, escalating from warning to alarm?
Does SPX reclaim 7,620 on a clean close (bull case) or confirm the ATH as a cycle peak by breaking 7,400 (bear case)?
Will the open hold above today's 7,516 intraday low, or gap lower into a continuation breakdown?
Does the 7,700-vs-7,400 Monte Carlo edge fall further below 65%, removing strong-conviction-long support?
Can RSI/MFI repair the bearish divergence, or do declining peaks confirm institutional distribution?

decision_matrix.valuation: Forward P/E 21.68x (Cautious 20–22x bucket); ERP ~+0.112% functionally zero — ceiling-adjacent
decision_matrix.technicals: Golden Cross intact, price above both SMAs (+10.1% / +14.6%); but ATH streak broken, distribution day, RSI ~63 off overbought, triple bearish MFI divergence
decision_matrix.sentiment: F&G 55 (Greed, 5th decline), Put/Call ~0.60 max complacency, breadth/strength in Fear, junk spreads Extreme Fear at 1.45%, VIX ~15
decision_matrix.risk: Leverage moderate; VIX below 20 (no elevated flag); near-term tripwire is 23.6% Fib at 7,330 (−3.4%), with 63% probability of being visited within 60 days

confirming_evidence: Trend regime intact: 50-day above 200-day, price above both SMAs (Golden Cross holds).
VIX ~15, below the 20 elevated-risk threshold — sole clean green signal; moving averages remain reliable.
Market Momentum component still Extreme Greed (SPX above 125-day MA) — primary trend not broken.
Primary Monte Carlo first-hit edge to Wave 1 (7,696) vs 38.2% Fib (7,151) = 77.3%, above the 65% threshold.
P(reclaim ATH 7,621 first vs 7,400) = 72.7% — upside still statistically favored near-term.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: SPX gained 65 pts net over the stretch and still printed a green close; trend regime intact.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: F&G fell from 61 (May 26) to 55 (June 4), a 5th consecutive decline, while price rose — one of the strongest negative price/sentiment divergences of the post-April rally; crowd less excited at higher prices.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Sentiment must confirm price; a falling F&G into rising/flat price is a fully-invested-market exhaustion signal — do not add.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Equities holding near ATH; VIX calm at ~15.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Junk bond spread pinned at 1.45% (Extreme Fear) for a 3rd consecutive day — April-shock levels that previously preceded a ~17% drawdown — while equity holds.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Spreads widening/holding stress while equity holds = credit market warning, institutional risk-off beginning; trim bias.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Price made new highs into June 2 and recovered intraday today.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: 3-month MFI shows three consecutive declining peaks against flat/rising price — a classic triple bearish divergence confirming weakening institutional money flow.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: MFI lower-high vs price higher-high is a trim-confirmation divergence; do not buy against weakening flow.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Primary Wave 1 first-hit edge remains 77.3%, comfortably above threshold.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Secondary 7,700-vs-7,400 contest degraded from 66.6% to 60.6% — below the 65% minimum — and P(23.6% Fib 7,330 in 60d) is now 63%.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Monte Carlo edge < 65% is insufficient — flag the secondary contest as monitor-only; conviction-long support is eroding.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Headline close was +0.41% green and price recovered the full intraday range.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Gap-down open to 7,516 (−93 pts), widest range of the stretch (81.6 pts), failed to reclaim June 2 close, and ATH streak broken — distribution day masquerading as a green close.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Distribution behavior after an exhausted rally shifts the burden of proof to bulls; the prior ATH at 7,620 is now resistance, not a floor.

primary_tension: A still-strong primary Monte Carlo melt-up edge (77.3% to Wave 1 at 7,696 first, only +1.47% away) versus a broad, five-session-deep stack of distribution and divergence signals — broken ATH streak, gap-down/failed-recovery distribution day, triple bearish MFI divergence, falling F&G into higher prices, persistent junk-bond Extreme Fear at April-shock levels, and a secondary MC contest (7,700 vs 7,400) that has degraded below the 65% threshold. The resolution typically favors VIX spiking to meet credit stress rather than credit compressing to meet equity complacency.

monte_carlo.conditional_cascade: P(7,700 first vs 7,400) degraded to 60.6% (below 65% threshold); P(reclaim ATH 7,621 first vs 7,400) 72.7%/27.3%; P(38.2% Fib 7,151 hit within 60d) 45.0% (rising); P(23.6% Fib 7,330 hit within 60d) 63.0% — new flag, more-likely-than-not visit.
monte_carlo.median_days: Wave 1 at 7,696 reachable within ~1-3 sessions on a rebound; 23.6% Fib 7,330 plausible in 1-3 sessions on a 2.5% follow-through down day.
